name: Deepgram
description: Speech AI platform: Nova speech-to-text (pre-recorded transcription with speaker diarization, summarization, language detection) and Aura text-to-speech (low-latency, natural voices in English and Spanish). REST API, single API key.

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Deepgram API Key
credential_fields.description: Create one at https://console.deepgram.com/project/default/keys. Speech calls need the normal request scopes; reporting tools need usage:read, and billing/balance/purchase tools need billing:read.

tools.name: speak
tools.description: Text-to-speech via Deepgram's Aura models. Converts text to natural-sounding audio at sub-second latency. Returns the generated audio as binary (mp3/wav/ogg depending on encoding). Best for conversational agents, voice notifications, and on-the-fly narration. Defaults to `aura-asteria-en` (English female); full model catalog at https://developers.deepgram.com/docs/tts-models.

text.description: The text to convert to speech. Max 2000 characters per request — longer inputs should be split client-side.

model.description: Voice model slug. Common choices: aura-asteria-en (female, conversational), aura-luna-en (female, warm), aura-stella-en (female, friendly), aura-athena-en (female, mature), aura-hera-en (female, deep), aura-orion-en (male, mature), aura-arcas-en (male, casual), aura-perseus-en (male, confident), aura-angus-en (male, Irish), aura-orpheus-en (male, narrator), aura-helios-en (male, upbeat), aura-zeus-en (male, authoritative), aura-sirio-es (Spanish male), aura-nestor-es (Spanish male). Also supports newer aura-2-* variants (e.g. aura-2-thalia-en) for higher quality.

encoding.description: Audio encoding. mp3 is the most compatible default. linear16 returns raw PCM — pair with `container=wav` to get a playable WAV file.

container.description: Audio container format. Most callers can leave this unset — mp3/linear16/flac pick sensible defaults. Required when encoding=linear16 and you want a WAV file.

sample_rate.description: Sample rate in Hz. Valid values depend on encoding: linear16/flac/mulaw/alaw accept 8000/16000/24000/32000/48000; mp3 is fixed at 22050; opus is fixed at 48000. Omit for the encoding-appropriate default.

bit_rate.description: Bit rate in bits per second for compressed encodings (mp3: 32000 or 48000, opus: 8000-32000, aac: 32000-128000). Omit for the default.

callback.description: Optional URL to receive the audio asynchronously instead of inline in the response. Deepgram POSTs the audio bytes to this URL when generation completes.

tools.name: listen
tools.description: Speech-to-text transcription of pre-recorded audio via Deepgram's Nova models (default: nova-3, their latest and most accurate, multilingual). Accepts the audio two ways — pick exactly one: (a) `url` for a publicly-reachable HTTPS link Deepgram will fetch (best for S3/CDN/public Drive links), or (b) `audio` as a blobref:// handle returned by an upstream download tool. By default you get ONLY the transcript text + paragraph-level structure; per-word timestamps are always stripped server-side (they're typically 90%+ of Deepgram's payload and not needed for analysis). Do NOT enable paragraphs/utterances/diarize/topics/intents/sentiment/summarize unless you specifically need that extra data — each one balloons the response and risks blowing the agent's context window on audio >5 minutes. Override the default via `model` for domain-tuned variants (nova-2-meeting, nova-2-phonecall) or Whisper.

url.description: Publicly-reachable HTTPS URL of the audio file to transcribe. Deepgram fetches this directly. Mutually exclusive with `audio`.

audio.description: A blobref:// handle produced by an upstream file-download tool (google-drive, dropbox, etc.). The runtime rehydrates the bytes and Deepgram receives them as the request body. Mutually exclusive with `url`. Do NOT base64-encode or inline raw bytes yourself.

model.description: STT model: nova-3 (latest, best accuracy + multilingual), nova-2 (prior generation, still excellent and cheaper), nova-2-meeting / nova-2-phonecall / nova-2-voicemail / nova-2-finance / nova-2-conversationalai (domain-tuned variants), enhanced, base, whisper-cloud.

language.description: BCP-47 language tag (en, en-US, es, fr, de, ja, zh, etc). Omit to default to English or use detect_language=true for auto-detection.

detect_language.description: Auto-detect the spoken language and include it in the response. Useful when you don't know the audio language up front.

smart_format.description: Apply readability formatting to transcripts: numbers, dates, times, URLs, emails, etc. Strongly recommended.

punctuate.description: Add punctuation and capitalization. Enabled by default when smart_format is on.

diarize.description: Identify speaker changes and label each word with a speaker number. Adds speaker labels per-word — BLOATS the response significantly. Leave off unless you specifically need speaker attribution in multi-speaker content (interviews, meetings).

paragraphs.description: Segment the transcript into paragraphs based on speaker changes and pauses. Adds a nested structure that duplicates the transcript text with per-sentence timestamps — roughly doubles response size. Leave off unless you specifically need paragraph boundaries.

utterances.description: Return per-utterance segments with start/end times. Adds another array re-serialising the same speech as time-stamped segments — bloats the response. Leave off unless you need per-utterance timing.

summarize.description: Generate a summary of the transcript. Use 'v2' for the current summarizer. Adds a summary field but usually small enough not to matter.

topics.description: Detect and label topics discussed in the audio. Adds a topics array — moderate response size impact.

intents.description: Detect speaker intents (requests, commitments, questions). Adds intents per utterance — moderate response size impact.

sentiment.description: Score sentiment per utterance (positive / neutral / negative). Adds sentiment labels per utterance — moderate response size impact.

filler_words.description: Include filler words like 'uh', 'um', 'you know' in the transcript. Off by default.

redact.description: Comma-separated entity types to redact: pci, numbers, ssn. E.g. 'pci,ssn' replaces detected sensitive data with [REDACTED].

keywords.description: Comma-separated keywords to boost in transcription accuracy. Use intensifier syntax for weight (e.g. 'apteva:2,agent:1').

callback.description: Optional URL for async delivery. Deepgram POSTs the full JSON result here instead of returning it inline.

tools.name: read
tools.description: Text Intelligence — runs analysis on plain text (no audio needed). Extracts topics, intents, sentiment, and summaries from a block of text or from a text file at a URL. Use for post-processing transcripts from other providers, analyzing emails, long documents, support tickets, etc. Same feature set as the transcript-analysis layer of listen but runs directly on text input.

text.description: The plain text to analyze. Mutually exclusive with url — supply exactly one.

url.description: HTTPS URL of a text document to analyze. Mutually exclusive with text.

summarize.description: Generate a summary. Use 'v2' for the current summarizer.

topics.description: Detect and label topics discussed in the text.

custom_topic.description: Comma-separated custom topics to detect (up to 100).

custom_topic_mode.description: strict = only match exact custom topics; extended = also surface related topics.

sentiment.description: Score sentiment across the text (positive / neutral / negative).

callback.description: Optional URL for async delivery of the analysis result.

callback_method.description: HTTP method Deepgram uses when calling back. POST by default.

tag.description: Comma-separated labels attached to this request for usage reporting and filtering.

tools.name: list_projects
tools.description: List every Deepgram project the API key has access to. Returns an array of `{project_id, name}` objects. Useful as the first call in any management flow — you need a project_id to list models, keys, usage, balances, members, etc.

tools.name: get_project
tools.description: Fetch a single project's metadata (name, company, created_at) by id. Usually called right after list_projects to confirm which project the agent is about to act on.

project_id.description: The project's unique identifier from list_projects.

tools.name: list_models
tools.description: List every speech-to-text (Nova / Whisper / enhanced / base variants) and text-to-speech (Aura voice) model enabled on a given project. Response groups models under `stt` and `tts` arrays with per-model metadata: name, version, supported languages, architecture, capability flags (batch/streaming/formatted_output), and for TTS: accent, age, voice sample URL. Use this before calling speak or listen to discover what's available and what the exact slug is.

include_outdated.description: Include prior-version models alongside the latest ones. Default false — you only see the currently-recommended models.

tools.name: get_model
tools.description: Full metadata for a single model (stt or tts) by its id within a project. Returns the same shape as an entry in list_models but with all optional fields populated — use when you need the exact supported languages, architecture, or voice sample URL for a specific model.

tools.name: get_usage
tools.description: Get raw Deepgram usage records for a project over a date range. Use this for detailed request-level usage auditing before summarizing cost. Dates use YYYY-MM-DD. Start with list_usage_fields to discover valid models, tags, features, and processing methods.

deployment.description: Filter by deployment type such as hosted, beta, self-hosted, or dedicated.

method.description: Filter by processing method, for example sync, async, or streaming.

punctuate.description: Filter by whether punctuate was enabled.

smart_format.description: Filter by whether smart_format was enabled.

diarize.description: Filter by whether diarize was enabled.

detect_language.description: Filter by whether language detection was enabled.

topics.description: Filter by whether topic detection was enabled.

intents.description: Filter by whether intent detection was enabled.

sentiment.description: Filter by whether sentiment analysis was enabled.

tools.name: get_usage_breakdown
tools.description: Aggregate Deepgram usage for a project by fields such as accessor, endpoint, feature_set, models, method, tags, and deployment. Use this to answer questions like which models or tags drove usage this month.

grouping.description: Fields to group by. Official values: accessor, endpoint, feature_set, models, method, tags, deployment.

tools.name: list_usage_fields
tools.description: List usage filter fields available for a project in a date range: tags, models, processing methods, and features. Call this before get_usage or get_usage_breakdown when an agent needs valid filter values.

tools.name: get_billing_breakdown
tools.description: Get Deepgram billing cost breakdown for a project. Results include USD dollar amounts, so this is the primary tool for questions like 'how much did Deepgram cost this month?' Use start/end dates in YYYY-MM-DD and optional grouping such as accessor, deployment, line_item, or tags.

grouping.description: Fields to group by. Official values include accessor, deployment, line_item, and tags.

tools.name: list_billing_fields
tools.description: List billing filter fields available for a project in a date range: accessors, deployments, tags, and line_items. Use this to discover valid filters before get_billing_breakdown.

tools.name: list_balances
tools.description: List outstanding Deepgram balances for a project, including amount, units, balance_id, and purchase_order_id.

tools.name: list_purchases
tools.description: List Deepgram project purchases and order transactions, including amount, units, created date, expiration, order_id, and order_type.

limit.description: Number of purchases to return. Default 10, maximum 1000.
